conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex, who's 9 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 9 years old.

Now I need to ask you a few questions to assess your eligibility:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

**Does Alex live with you, or does he live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for that information.

Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex currently in hospital, in care, or in any residential accommodation?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in hospital or care or anything like that. He's just at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

One final question: **Does Alex attend school or are there any special circumstances with his education or living situation that I should know about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He just goes to primary school - nothing special, just regular school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the information you've provided, let me evaluate your situation:

- You have a child, Alex, who is 9 years old (under 16)
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- Alex lives with you at home
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation
- Alex attends regular primary school

According to the UK government rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them, which you do.

**You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
You should be able to apply for Child Benefit for Alex. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 9 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), attends regular primary school, is not in hospital or care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. The claimant is responsible for Alex as they live together. According to gov.uk guidance, a person normally qualifies for Child Benefit if they're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK, and they're usually responsible if they live with the child. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 9-year-old son Alex who attends primary school. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and he is not in hospital or care. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

The claimant is responsible for Alex as they live together. According to gov.uk guidance, a person normally qualifies for Child Benefit if they're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK, and they're usually responsible if they live with the child. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 9-year-old son Alex who attends primary school. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and he is not in hospital or care. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
